top_stories.title: Cisco Patches Four Critical Identity Services, Webex Flaws Enabling Code Execution
top_stories.summary: Cisco has released patches for four critical vulnerabilities affecting its Identity Services Engine (ISE) and Webex, with CVSS scores reaching 9.8. The flaws include improper certificate validation in SSO integration that could allow attackers to impersonate any user within the service, alongside other vulnerabilities enabling arbitrary code execution.

top_stories.title: The death of standing privilege in the age of AI agents
top_stories.summary: AI agents are emerging as highly privileged identities that traditional Privileged Access Management (PAM) controls are not designed to handle. The article addresses the security gap created by standing privilege models when applied to autonomous AI agents, which require dynamic, just-in-time access approaches instead.

top_stories.title: SailPoint、AWSとの戦略的協業契約を締結　ユニファイド アイデンティティ ガバナンス レイヤーによるAIエージェントの保護を可能に
top_stories.summary: SailPoint has announced a strategic collaboration agreement with AWS to enable protection of AI agents through a unified identity governance layer. The partnership focuses on extending SailPoint's identity governance capabilities to secure agentic AI systems within AWS environments.

top_stories.title: 5 Ways Zero Trust Maximizes Identity Security
top_stories.summary: Specops outlines five ways a Zero Trust architecture can strengthen identity security, focusing on credential theft prevention and privilege escalation mitigation. The article covers access limitation, device trust enforcement, and lateral movement blocking as key Zero Trust identity controls.

top_stories.title: Cisco just made moves to own the AI infrastructure stack
top_stories.summary: Cisco acquired Astrix Security to bolster its identity security capabilities as part of a broader strategy to own the AI infrastructure stack. The move positions Cisco to manage identity and access controls for agentic AI systems, adding non-human identity and API security to its portfolio.

news.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free from the Desktop
news.summary: The article discusses extending two-factor authentication (2FA) beyond traditional desktop/IT environments to address physical security threats. It explores implementing 2FA as an additional security barrier in non-traditional settings where threat actors can bypass conventional security systems.

news.title: [Webinar] Find and Eliminate Orphaned Non-Human Identities in Your Environment
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted on finding and eliminating orphaned non-human identities, including service accounts, API tokens, and OAuth grants. The session addresses the management of non-human identities, citing that compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were responsible for 68% of cloud breaches in 2024.

news.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
news.summary: Microsoft and Salesforce patched prompt injection vulnerabilities in their respective AI agents, Copilot and Agentforce, that could have allowed external attackers to leak sensitive data. The flaws relate to AI agent security rather than core IAM functionality, though they touch on access control boundaries within AI-assisted enterprise tools.

news.title: Capsule Security debuts with $7 million funding to secure AI agent behavior
news.summary: Capsule Security has emerged from stealth with $7 million in seed funding to provide security controls for AI agent behavior. The company focuses on preventing AI agents from being manipulated or exfiltrating data, touching on access and authorization concerns in agentic AI contexts.

news.title: Privilege Elevation Dominates Massive Microsoft Patch Update
news.summary: Microsoft released a major patch update addressing 165 vulnerabilities, with elevation-of-privilege bugs comprising more than half of the total. Two zero-day vulnerabilities were included among the privilege escalation flaws patched in the update.

news.title: TechJutsu launches browser extension to bring MFA to the web
news.summary: TechJutsu has launched a browser extension designed to bring multi-factor authentication (MFA) capabilities to web-based applications. No additional details about the product's specific features, target market, or technical implementation are provided in the description.

news.title: Show HN: Prmana – OIDC SSH Login for Linux with DPoP (Rust, Apache 2.0)
news.summary: Prmana is an open-source tool (Rust, Apache 2.0) that replaces static SSH keys with short-lived OIDC tokens validated at the host via PAM. The implementation uses DPoP (RFC 9449) to cryptographically bind tokens to the key holder, differentiating it from other OIDC-for-SSH approaches.

news.title: APT41 Delivers 'Zero-Detection' Backdoor to Harvest Cloud Credentials
news.summary: APT41, a China-backed threat group, has deployed a "zero-detection" backdoor specifically designed to harvest cloud credentials from AWS, Google, Azure, and Alibaba environments. The attack uses typosquatting techniques to obscure command-and-control communication while targeting cloud identity credentials.

news.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
news.summary: The FBI and Indonesian National Police dismantled the W3LL phishing network, arresting its alleged developer and disrupting infrastructure used to steal account credentials and attempt over $20 million in fraud. The W3LL toolkit was an off-the-shelf phishing kit designed to bypass MFA and compromise Microsoft 365 accounts at scale.

news.title: Bringing governance and visibility to machine and AI identities
news.summary: AppViewX CEO Archit Lohokare discusses the convergence of machine and AI agent identity management as a unified governance challenge. The interview draws on his experience at IBM and CyberArk to address visibility and governance approaches for non-human identities.

news.title: Your Next Breach Will Look Like Business as Usual
news.summary: The article addresses the growing threat of credential-based attacks and the detection model changes cybersecurity teams need to adopt to counter them. The focus is on identifying malicious activity that mimics legitimate user behavior, a core challenge in identity and access management.

news.title: Russia's 'Fancy Bear' APT Continues Its Global Onslaught
news.summary: The article covers ongoing cyberattacks by Russia's Fancy Bear (APT28) threat group targeting global organizations. Security experts recommend patching and zero trust architecture as essential defensive measures against the group's espionage campaigns.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.

news.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
news.summary: A new phishing-as-a-service platform called "Starkiller" proxies real login pages in real time, relaying victims' credentials and MFA codes to legitimate sites rather than using static page copies. The service effectively bypasses MFA protections by acting as a man-in-the-middle between the victim and the authentic website, capturing valid session tokens in the process.
